column1: # Product: Canara HSBC Secure Growth Plan
# Generated by: Senior Technical QA Engineer

column5: Pre-Requisites
column6: Test Scenario / Objective
column8: Steps Description
column9: Expected Result

column5: User is logged into the Insure portal and is on the New Business journey page.
column6: Verify that the new product 'Canara HSBC Secure Growth Plan' is available for selection.
column8:
1. Navigate to the product selection dropdown.
2. Search for or scroll to find the new product.
column9: The product 'Canara HSBC Secure Growth Plan' should be listed and selectable in the product dropdown.
column10: User is able to select the product and proceed to the next step.

column5: 'Canara HSBC Secure Growth Plan' is selected. User is on the customer details screen.
column6: Verify that all mandatory fields are visually indicated as required (e.g., with an asterisk '*').
column8: 1. Observe the fields: Full Name, DOB, Gender, Mobile No, Email ID, PAN.
column9: An asterisk (*) or similar mandatory indicator should be present next to the label of each of these fields.

column5: User is on the customer details screen.
column6: Verify that the 'Age' field is auto-calculated correctly based on the Date of Birth (DOB) entered.
column8:
1. Select a DOB from the date picker: 01-Jan-1990.
2. Tab out or click outside the DOB field.
column9: The 'Age' field should be automatically populated with the correct calculated age (e.g., 34 years, depending on the current date) and should be non-editable.
column10: Age is correctly calculated for further validations.

column5: User has filled all other details on the page but left the 'Full Name' field blank.
column6: Verify that submission is blocked if a mandatory field ('Full Name') is empty.
column8:
1. Fill all details on the customer/plan page except 'Full Name'.
2. Click the 'Submit' or 'Proceed' button.
column9: An inline error message 'Full Name is required' should appear next to the field. The page submission should be blocked.
column10: User remains on the same page until the validation error is fixed.

column5: User is on the customer details screen.
column6: Verify validation on 'Mobile No' field for non-numeric input.
column8:
1. Enter 'abcdefghij' in the 'Mobile No' field.
2. Tab out of the field.
column9: An error message 'Mobile number must be numeric' should be displayed. The input should either be rejected or flagged as invalid.

column5: User is on the customer details screen.
column6: Verify validation on 'Mobile No' field for incorrect length (less than 10 digits).
column8:
1. Enter '987654321' (9 digits) in the 'Mobile No' field.
2. Click 'Submit'.
column9: An error message 'Mobile number must be 10 digits' should be displayed. Submission is blocked.

column5: User is on the customer details screen.
column6: Verify validation on 'Email ID' for invalid format.
column8:
1. Enter 'test@domain' in the 'Email ID' field.
2. Click 'Submit'.
column9: An error message 'Please enter a valid email address' should be displayed. Submission is blocked.

column5: User is on the customer details screen.
column6: Verify validation on 'PAN' for incorrect format.
column8:
1. Enter 'ABC1234P' (invalid format) in the 'PAN' field.
2. Click 'Submit'.
column9: An error message 'Please enter a valid PAN' should be displayed. Submission is blocked.

column5: User is on the plan details screen.
column6: Verify system accepts entry age at the minimum boundary (25 years).
column8:
1. Enter a DOB that calculates the age to exactly 25 years.
2. Proceed to the next step or calculate premium.
column9: The system should accept the age without any validation error.

column5: User is on the plan details screen.
column6: Verify system accepts entry age at the maximum boundary (55 years).
column8:
1. Enter a DOB that calculates the age to exactly 55 years.
2. Proceed to the next step or calculate premium.
column9: The system should accept the age without any validation error.

column5: User is on the plan details screen.
column6: Verify system rejects entry age below the minimum boundary (24 years).
column8:
1. Enter a DOB that calculates the age to 24 years.
2. Tab out of the DOB field.
column9: An error message 'Entry age must be between 25 and 55 years' must be displayed.
column10: Progression to the next step is blocked.

column5: User is on the plan details screen.
column6: Verify system rejects entry age above the maximum boundary (56 years).
column8:
1. Enter a DOB that calculates the age to 56 years.
2. Tab out of the DOB field.
column9: An error message 'Entry age must be between 25 and 55 years' must be displayed.
column10: Progression to the next step is blocked.

column5: User is on the plan details screen.
column6: Verify the Policy Term (PT) dropdown contains the correct fixed values.
column8: 1. Click on the 'Policy Term' dropdown.
column9: The dropdown should display only three options: 15, 20, 25.

column5: User is on the plan details screen.
column6: Verify that the PPT dropdown options are correctly filtered based on the selected PT.
column8:
1. Select Policy Term (PT) as '15'.
2. Click on the 'Premium Payment Term' (PPT) dropdown.
column9:
The PPT dropdown should contain the options: 7, 10, 12.
3. Select PT as '20'.
4. Click on the PPT dropdown again.
column10: The PPT dropdown should still contain the options: 7, 10, 12. (As per BRD 3.4, PPT < PT is always true for all combinations).

column5: User is on the plan details screen.
column6: Verify system accepts Sum Assured at the minimum boundary (₹5,00,000).
column8:
1. Enter '500000' in the 'Sum Assured' field.
2. Click 'Calculate Premium'.
column9: The system should accept the value and calculate the premium.

column5: User is on the plan details screen.
column6: Verify system accepts Sum Assured at the maximum boundary (₹75,00,000).
column8:
1. Enter '7500000' in the 'Sum Assured' field.
2. Click 'Calculate Premium'.
column9: The system should accept the value and calculate the premium.

column5: User is on the plan details screen.
column6: Verify system rejects Sum Assured below the minimum boundary.
column8:
1. Enter '499999' in the 'Sum Assured' field.
2. Tab out or click 'Calculate Premium'.
column9: An error message 'Sum Assured must be between ₹5,00,000 and ₹75,00,000' should be displayed.

column5: User is on the plan details screen.
column6: Verify system rejects Sum Assured that is not in multiples of ₹50,000.
column8:
1. Enter '510005' in the 'Sum Assured' field.
2. Tab out or click 'Calculate Premium'.
column9: An error message 'Sum Assured must be in multiples of ₹50,000' should be displayed.

column5: All mandatory fields are filled with valid data.
column6: Verify that clicking 'Calculate Premium' triggers a calculation and displays the premium breakdown.
column8:
1. Enter all valid data for customer and plan parameters.
2. Click the 'Calculate Premium' button.
column9: The UI should display non-zero, calculated values for 'Base Premium', 'GST', and 'Total Premium'. The fields should update from their default state (e.g., 0 or empty).
column10: Premium is calculated and displayed for user review.

column5: All mandatory fields are filled. Premium is calculated.
column6: Verify that checking the 'Bank/Insurer Staff' checkbox applies the discount and updates the premium.
column8:
1. Enter valid data and click 'Calculate Premium'. Note the 'Total Premium' (e.g., P1).
2. Check the 'Bank/Insurer Staff' checkbox.
3. Click 'Calculate Premium' again. Note the new 'Total Premium' (e.g., P2).
column9: The premium summary should now show a 'Staff Discount' line item. The new Total Premium (P2) should be less than the original premium (P1). The discount amount should be 7.5% of the base premium.

column5: Staff discount is applied.
column6: Verify that unchecking the 'Bank/Insurer Staff' checkbox removes the discount.
column8:
1. Follow steps for INS_UI_020 to apply the discount.
2. Uncheck the 'Bank/Insurer Staff' checkbox.
3. Click 'Calculate Premium' again.
column9: The 'Staff Discount' line item should disappear from the premium summary. The 'Total Premium' should revert to the original, non-discounted value (P1).

column5: Base plan details are entered. SA is ₹10,00,000.
column6: Verify that adding a rider updates the total premium.
column8:
1. Enter base plan details and calculate premium. Note the Total Premium.
2. Select the 'Accidental Death Benefit (ADB)' rider checkbox.
3. Enter a valid Rider SA (e.g., ₹10,00,000).
4. Click 'Calculate Premium'.
column9: The UI should now display a 'Rider Premium' value in the summary. The 'Total Premium' should be higher than the base premium alone.
column10: Rider premium is added to the total quote.

column5: Base plan details are entered. SA is ₹10,00,000.
column6: Verify validation that Rider SA cannot exceed Base SA.
column8:
1. Enter Base SA as ₹10,00,000.
2. Select the 'ADB' rider.
3. Enter Rider SA as ₹10,00,001.
4. Click 'Calculate Premium'.
column9: An error message 'Rider Sum Assured cannot exceed Base Sum Assured' should be displayed. Premium calculation should be blocked or show an error state.

column5: User has filled all details for a valid, standard case without any negative parameters.
column6: Verify the end-to-end happy path flow from data entry to successful submission.
column8:
1. Login to Insure portal.
2. Select 'Canara HSBC Secure Growth Plan'.
3. Fill all Proposer and Life Assured details correctly. (Age: 40)
4. Select PT=20, PPT=10, SA=₹15,00,000.
5. Click 'Calculate Premium'. Verify premium is displayed.
6. Click 'Submit'.
column9:
1. All validations should pass without any error.
2. A success message 'Application created successfully. Your Application Number is [ANxxxxxxxx]' should be displayed.
3. The page may redirect to a confirmation screen or payment gateway.
column10: An Application Number is generated. The application data is successfully pushed to the downstream system (nVEST/UW).
column11: User credentials, Full set of valid customer & plan data.

column5: User has filled all details but one field has an invalid value (e.g., age is 24).
column6: Verify that 'Submit' re-triggers all validations and blocks submission on error.
column8:
1. Fill all details correctly, but enter a DOB that results in an age of 24.
2. Bypass the initial field-level validation if possible and click 'Submit'.
column9: The system should re-validate all data. An error message 'Entry age must be between 25 and 55 years' must be displayed. The submission must be blocked.
column10: User remains on the data entry page with the error highlighted. No Application Number is generated.

column5: All mandatory fields are filled with valid data.
column6: Verify that the premium calculation API call fails gracefully if the service is down.
column8:
1. Use browser developer tools to mock a 500 server error for the premium calculation API endpoint.
2. Click 'Calculate Premium'.
column9: The UI should not crash. It should display a user-friendly error message like 'Unable to calculate premium at the moment. Please try again later.' The premium fields should not be populated.
column11: Requires developer tools (e.g., Chrome DevTools) to mock API response.
